title: Boat Rental in Bazaruto: Costs, Seasons and Tips for 2026
html:
Renting a boat in Bazaruto gives you access to one of Africa’s finest marine playgrounds, starting from Vilankulo harbour with prices from €350 for a half-day excursion. To find the perfect vessel, use BoatHire24 to compare verified boat listings and check availability directly.

Where to set off in Bazaruto

Most charters depart from Vilankulo, the mainland gateway to the Bazaruto Archipelago, a 40-minute boat ride to Bazaruto Island itself. Other launching points include the jetty at the Bazaruto Archipelago National Park headquarters on Benguerra Island, the sheltered bay at Magaruque Island, and the tiny fishing village of Linga Linga on the mainland shore. Each offers a different perspective, from uninhabited dunes to thriving coral reefs.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motorboat (e.g. Axopar 28) Best for fast island hopping and day trips, up to 8 guests, from €450 half-day.
  2. Sailing catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 450) Ideal for relaxed overnight charters with a family or group, up to 10 guests, from €1,200 full-day.
  3. Luxury motor yacht (e.g. Maiora 26m) Perfect for longer expeditions with crew and service, up to 12 guests; for detailed specs see this luxury yacht.
  4. Traditional dhow A unique local experience for a sunset cruise, up to 6 guests, from €200 for two hours.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Bazaruto?

Prices vary widely from €200 for a short dhow trip to over €3,000 for a crewed luxury yacht for a day. Here is a typical price breakdown:

  • Shared snorkelling tour: from €80 per person, including lunch.
  • Small motorboat half-day (up to 4 people): from €350.
  • Catamaran full-day (up to 10 people): from €1,200.
  • Crewed yacht full-day (up to 12 people, inclusive of skipper and crew): from €2,500.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Bazaruto?

For bareboat (self-drive) rental you generally need a recognised boating licence, but most operators insist on a professional skipper for safety. The Mozambican authorities do not issue a separate local licence; they accept an International Certificate of Competence or equivalent. Always confirm exact requirements with the operator.

During the high season from May to November the southeasterly trade wind, known locally as the ‘Bazaruto breeze’, makes sailing conditions steady and the water visibility excellent for diving.

When is the best time to rent a boat in Bazaruto?

The peak season runs from May to November, when the weather is dry and seas are calm. December through March brings heat and occasional rain, but also fewer crowds and lower prices. April and May see the transition period with pleasant conditions. Booking early for June, July and August is recommended.

At a glance

  • Price band: €200 to €3,000+ per day.
  • Peak months: May to November.
  • Trip length: Half-day to several days.
  • Capacity: 4 to 12 guests on most boats.
  • Currency used: Euro (EUR).

Questions about boat rental in Bazaruto

Can I rent a boat for a day trip to Bazaruto Island?

Yes, a day trip from Vilankulo typically takes 30, 40 minutes to reach the island and includes time for snorkelling, lunch and exploring the dunes.

Is it possible to charter a catamaran for a week?

Yes, several operators offer weekly catamaran charters based out of Vilankulo, with itineraries covering the whole archipelago and nearby Santa Carolina.

Do I need to bring my own snorkel gear?

Most rental boats include snorkelling equipment, but it is wise to bring your own mask for a better fit, as the reefs are among the best in the Indian Ocean.

What happens if the weather is bad on my charter day?

Operators monitor conditions closely; if the sea is too rough, they typically offer a rescheduled date or a full refund, especially during the summer storm months.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Bazaruto or list your own. If you own a boat in the region, you can list a boat for free and reach thousands of travellers planning their 2026 escape.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Bazaruto and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
